agent_outputs.raw: The AutoML benchmark has three methodological flaws that together invalidate the claimed advantage. First, target encoding was fitted globally before fold creation — this is preprocessing leakage, not a critique of target encoding as a method. Second, the threshold was selected on the held-out test set, making the precision estimate at that threshold in-sample optimistic. Third, the baseline comparison is not controlled — it uses a different preprocessing pipeline and threshold selection protocol. Each of these problems individually would require correction; together they make the reported AutoML advantage uninterpretable. Critique wins.

I see the classic preprocessing leakage error: global feature fitting before fold boundaries. Target encoding for assessor ID or repair shop encodes the mean of the binary claim investigation outcome for each value. When this encoding is applied to the full dataset before cross-validation, each fold's evaluation sees feature values that encode information from that fold's outcomes. The resulting CV AUC is inflated. Additionally, the threshold for 80% recall was found on the test set, not the validation set. And the baseline is under a different protocol. Three independent problems, all inflating or distorting the AutoML advantage estimate. The steering committee should reject this benchmark.

The benchmark cannot support the implementation decision. Preprocessing leakage (target encoding before fold creation), test-set threshold contamination, and asymmetric baseline protocol are three separate methodological errors. The correct approach — fold-specific target encoding, validation-set threshold selection for both systems, and evaluation on an independent test set — is well-established. Until the corrected evaluation is available, the magnitude of the AutoML advantage is unknown. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents identify the same three flaws with consistent reasoning. The benchmark must be corrected before the implementation decision.

etd_constraint.condition: Fold-specific target encoding; validation-set threshold selection for both AutoML and baseline; evaluation on independent test set
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: AutoML lift at 80% recall falls below 1 percentage point or ROC-AUC advantage shrinks below 0.01
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: AutoML still shows 0.02+ ROC-AUC improvement and 3+ point precision gain under leakage-free conditions
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC gain preserved but threshold-level precision advantage disappears

all_issues_raised: Global target encoding before fold creation — leakage (all three agents)
Test-set threshold selection — precision estimate contaminated (all three agents)
